subject: chemistry
title: 22M.1.HL.TZ2.16
question_html:
Which equation represents the bond enthalpy for H–Br in hydrogen bromide?

A.  HBr (g) → H+ (g) + Br− (g)

B.  HBr (g) → H (g) + Br (g)

C.  HBr (g) → 12H2 (g) + 12Br2 (l)

D.  HBr (g) → 12H2 (g) + 12Br2 (g)
examiners_html: Only a quarter of the candidates identified the equation representing the bond enthalpy of HBr. The most commonly chosen distractor had the elements H2 and Br2 as the products.
